Category:
Management (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Hybrid work environment with flexible arrangements
5-year Fixed Term Contract
Play a key role in shaping the future of knowledge and information at the RBA
Lead a high-performing team in a purpose-driven organisation


About the business/role/team

As Senior Manager Knowledge Services, you will report to the Head of Knowledge Management and lead a team responsible for delivering enterprise-wide knowledge services. You will work closely with colleagues across the Bank, including project teams, technology partners, and business units, to embed best practice knowledge, information and data management principles into systems, processes and culture.

About the role


Lead the development and implementation of a knowledge management framework across the Bank
Guide and coordinate a strategy for enterprise-wide knowledge, culture, assets and services
Build and lead a team and coalition of resources to deliver high-impact Knowledge Services
Provide strategic advice and operational support for projects with a knowledge component
Champion collaboration and continuous improvement in work practices, systems and culture
Partner with stakeholders across the Bank to support delivery of critical knowledge initiatives
Contribute to the leadership and strategic direction of the Knowledge Management Department


About you


Proven experience leading knowledge management functions in complex organisations
Strong strategic thinking and ability to influence and collaborate across all levels
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Deep understanding of collaborative and information platforms and their potential
Demonstrated ability to lead capability development and cultural change initiatives
Analytical mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and innovation


Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.


Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

The Opportunity
Our client is a powerhouse in the Australian industrial services sector, supporting critical industries from mining to agriculture with a national footprint of over 1,000 employees. They are currently executing a high-stakes, multi-year Finance Transformation Program designed to modernize their systems and create an insight-led finance model.
  
We are looking for a Finance Technology Specialist who can act as the vital "translator" between the Finance, Technology, and PMO departments. This is not a role for those who prefer to stay on the sidelines; you will be the system-side authority driving the design and delivery of their ERP roadmap.
  
Key Responsibilities
Serve as the Finance Design Authority for all ERP and technology implementations.
  
Drive hands-on system configuration and set-up to ensure technology meets complex accounting requirements.
  
Lead the definition of functional specifications, data structures, and reporting hierarchies.
  
Own the Finance-side of project delivery, including User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and data migration validation.
  
Identify and implement design-led improvements to reduce manual rework and strengthen internal controls.
  
Influence and align stakeholders across more than 50 locations to adopt new ways of working.
  
The Ideal Candidate

The successful candidate will be a rare blend: someone with a "Big Picture" design mindset who isn't afraid to get into the weeds of configuration.

  ERP Expertise (Mandatory): Extensive experience with ERP implementations is required, with a heavy preference for candidates with hands-on Infor LN experience.
    
  Financial Foundation: You possess a strong accounting background and a deep understanding of financial reporting, controls, and core modules like GL, AP, AR, and Fixed Assets.
  
  Academic Background: You hold a Bachelor’s degree in Commerce or Technology.
    
  Transformation Savvy: Experience in complex, multi-entity, or acquisitive environments where you have driven operating model change is highly desirable.  
  
  Tech Stack: Proficiency in data and reporting tools such as Power BI, SQL, or advanced Excel is a distinct advantage.
Why Apply?

If you are looking for a role where you can apply best-practice thinking to a rapidly growing organization, this is it. You will have the authority to challenge legacy practices and play a central role in building a modern, integrated industrial business.
  
  
To apply or learn more about this transformation-focused role, please submit your CV for a confidential discussion.

Salary: Base Salary | 14% Super | Bonus | Leisure Days
Ad Text:
Shape Your World

At Alcoa, you will become an essential part of our purpose: to turn raw potential into real progress. The way we see it, every Alcoan is a work-shaper, team-shaper, idea-shaper & world-shaper.

As a leader within Alcoa, you can help fulfill our purpose and realise our vision to build a legacy of excellence for future generations. Be part of the team that is helping shape a better workplace with the flexibility and equal opportunities that help everyone thrive. You have the power to shape things and individuals to make them better.

About the Role

Are you a strategic thinker with a passion for turning data into powerful business insights? Join Alcoa’s WA Mining team as a Principal ABS – Performance Insights and play a pivotal role in shaping data-driven decision making across our WA Mining Operations operations. This role is offered on a Monday to Friday roster based at our Mining Hub in Pinjarra.

This is a key leadership position where you will move beyond reporting to deliver predictive insights, performance optimisation and strategic direction, helping drive operational excellence and long-term business success

As the Principal ABS – Performance Insights, you will:

Define and execute the analytics roadmap aligned to business strategy and priorities.

Lead analysis of complex datasets to identify trends, risks and opportunities.

Translate data into clear, actionable insights that drive performance improvement.

Partner with senior leaders as a trusted advisor, influencing decision making.

Establish best practice in data governance, reporting and analytics processes.

Lead, coach and mentor others, building capability and a culture of continuous improvement.

You will play a critical role in transforming raw data into meaningful narratives that guide strategic planning, operational efficiency and future growth

What’s on offer

Flexible hybrid working options available.

18 weeks Parental leave for primary caregivers.

One additional paid Alcoa Flexi Day.

Alcoa Live Well program offering travel, lifestyle, health and wellbeing discounts.

Generous leave entitlements including a leisure a day off every 4-week period.

Salary packaging for a novated car lease, employee share plan and superannuation options.

What you can bring to the role

Tertiary qualification in Engineering, Analytics, Business Intelligence or similar, coupled with significant experience in a data-driven or analytics-focused role.

Proven experience in a senior or leadership capacity.

Strong technical capability in SQL, Python or R, and BI tools such as Power BI or Tableau.

Exceptional communication, influencing and stakeholder engagement skills.

Experience within mining or heavy industry (highly desirable).

Additional information

Interviews may progress prior to the closing date, although all applications will be considered.

You will only be contacted if you are shortlisted for an interview, this process can take up to four weeks from the closing date.

Be part of shaping our organisation and join us in building a legacy of excellence for future generations.

#AlcoaAUS

#LI-SV1  

About the Location

Huntly is one of our two bauxite mines in Western Australia. Established in 1976 and located east of North Dandalup in WA, it is the world’s second largest bauxite mine, supplying ore to Alcoa’s Pinjarra alumina refinery. Bauxite ore from Alcoa’s Huntly and Willowdale mines helps to produce almost half of Australia’s alumina and approximately 19 per cent of Australia’s aluminium. Our workplace is an inclusive and respectful environment, where we embrace change, new ideas and equal opportunity to succeed. 

We are values led, vision driven and united by our purpose of transforming raw potential into real progress.  Our commitments to Inclusion, Diversity & Equity include providing trusting workplaces that are safe, respectful and inclusive of all individuals, free from discrimination, bullying and harassment and that our workplaces reflect the diversity of the communities in which we operate.    

This is a place where you are empowered to do your best work, be your authentic self, and feel a true sense of belonging. Come join us and shape your career!  

Your work. Your world. Shape them for the better.

Job Title: Senior Manager, Information and Data Governance
Category:
Management (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
Hybrid work environment - 50% Office/Home
5-year Fixed Term Contract
Play an important part shaping the future of our iconic Australian institution


About the business/role/team

As Senior Manager, Information and Data Governance, you will report to the Deputy Head of Knowledge Management and lead a team of specialists across Information and Data Governance, Compliance and Architecture teams, where you will work closely with senior leaders in Risk, Technology, and Business Units to embed governance principles and enable data-driven decision-making.

This team sits within the Bank's Knowledge Management Department, which is central to enterprise information strategy and compliance.

About the role


Manage enterprise Information, Data, and AI governance frameworks, aligned with Commonwealth legislative requirements and Bank policy.
Manage the Bank's information classification framework, ensuring sensitive information is appropriately classified, handled, and protected, and maintain alignment with the Protective Security Policy Framework (PSPF), Australian Government Information Security Manual (ISM), and internal security standards.
Ensure compliance with recordkeeping obligations, including the Archives Act and relevant standards for digital and physical records.
Ensure that data models, standards, and platforms align with governance, security, and compliance requirements.
Partner with technology and business leaders to ensure governance supports innovation, analytics, and AI adoption while mitigating risk.
Champion cultural change by promoting information and data stewardship, security awareness, and information integrity across the organisation.


About you


Deep expertise in information and data governance, including regulatory compliance, risk management, and emerging AI governance practices.
Strong knowledge of Commonwealth information security and recordkeeping frameworks, including PSPF, ISM, and Archives Act requirements.
Proven leadership experience in complex organisations, ideally in financial services or government.
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence at all levels and drive enterprise-wide adoption of governance principles.
Familiarity with data platforms, metadata management, records management and governance technologies.


Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

About Lactalis-Mainland Dairy


Mainland Dairy, the business formerly known as Fonterra’s global Consumer and associated businesses, is now part of the Lactalis Group and known as Lactalis-Mainland Dairy. 


Lactalis is a French family-owned business founded in Laval in 1933. Today, it is the world’s leading dairy group, present in 50 countries employing around 85,500 people. It is home to some of the world’s best known global and international dairy brands, including Président, Galbani and Parmalat, and local brands including Pauls, OAK, and Cracker Barrel in Australia.  


Lactalis-Mainland Dairy is a team of more than 4,300 employees, operating 16 manufacturing sites across Oceania, Southeast Asia, South Asia and the Middle East. We make well-loved brands including Perfect Italiano, Mainland, and Western Star. 


Our passionate and talented team is committed to nurturing the future, unlocking new opportunities, and fostering innovation in nutrition and food.  


The Opportunity


Reporting to the Finance System Compliance Specialist, the Finance System Reporting Lead is responsible for the ownership, stability and integrity of finance systems and system driven reporting across the organisation.


This role plays a pivotal part in ensuring accurate, timely and reliable financial outputs across management reporting, allocations, forecasting and consolidation. Acting as a key link between Finance, IT and business stakeholders, you will support month end and planning cycles while enabling effective decision making through high quality system reporting and insights.


A core element of the role involves close collaboration with offshore Hub teams, Group and Regional FP&A, ensuring accuracy of Hub prepared reports and alignment during ongoing management reporting system transition.


In this role, you will:


Own and administer core finance systems (SAP ECC/S/4, Central Finance and planning/forecasting tools), ensuring stability, accuracy and strong data governance 
Manage allocation, settlement and close cycle processes (actuals and forecast) to support management reporting and decision making 
Partner closely with Finance, FP&A, IT and offshore Hub teams to resolve system issues, support transitions and ensure high quality reporting outputs 
Deliver monthly management reporting, insights and analysis on revenue, cost and margin performance 
Support budgeting, forecasting, audit requirements and continuous improvement across finance systems and reporting processes


About You


You are a finance system–focused professional who enjoys working at the intersection of systems, data and commercial insight. You bring a collaborative mindset, strong technical capability and the confidence to work with stakeholders across Finance, IT and the wider business.


To succeed in this role, you’ll ideally bring:


A degree in Accounting, Finance or a related discipline, with CA/CPA (or equivalent) preferred 
Strong experience supporting management reporting, allocations, forecasting and close processes within complex finance systems 
Hands on knowledge of SAP ECC/S/4, Central Finance and planning or forecasting tools (FICO/COPA experience highly regarded) 
Well developed analytical and problem solving skills, with the ability to translate financial data into clear insights 
Strong communication skills and the ability to operate effectively in a matrixed, multinational environment


What We Offer


Competitive pay and benefits
Career development and internal opportunities across a global business
A team focused workplace in a fantastic culture where your work has real impact
A strong commitment to quality, innovation and sustainability
Flexible work arrangements where possible


Based at our Richmond office, this role offers excellent career development and internal mobility pathways as well as flexible ways of working and a hybrid structure, that allows you the balance of in-office collaboration and working from home to suit your lifestyle. 


Please note that applicants must have full working rights for Australia to be considered.
We are politely declining recruitment agency support and approaches for this role.

Company: Department of Transport and Main Roads
Ad Text:
Are you a strategic leader with a passion for data, reporting and shaping major infrastructure programs? In the role of Manager (Reporting), you will lead the delivery of high quality reporting and analysis for the Queensland Transport and Roads Investment Program (QTRIP), supporting key state and federal budget processes, Estimates, and whole of government performance requirements. You'll provide expert advice to senior leaders, drive improvements in reporting frameworks and data quality, and deliver clear, insightful analysis that informs decision making.
This role offers the opportunity to influence how the department monitors and communicates program performance, while building capability, leading a high performing team, and contributing to outcomes that benefit communities across Queensland.

In the role of Manager (Reporting) you will lead and provide direction on the reporting and analysis for the department's annual Queensland Transport and Roads Investment Program (Q TRIP) publication, including preparing and providing information for state and federal budgetary processes, Estimates Committee Hearings, and whole-of-government program performance reporting requirements. You will provide high level strategic advice, expertise and leadership in relation to program reporting for the department.

Some of the key responsibilities for this role include:

Lead and provide direction for the preparation and delivery of the QTRIP and State Budget documentation, including Budget Paper 3 — Capital Statement (BP3), Regional Delivery Plans (RDPS) and Service Delivery Statement (SDS), as well as the department's Annual Report.
Lead and manage the preparation of insightful program performance analysis for delivery areas and senior executives and respond to internal and external reporting needs, including the Transport Infrastructure Portfolio performance and the whole-of-government Capital Program performance. This includes developing reporting and dashboard processes and templates, procedures and guidelines, extracting relevant financial and non-financial data, and conducting data verification activities.

We recognise and celebrate that everyone is unique and seek the applicant best suited to the role. We will assess your experience, knowledge and acquired skills, as well as your potential for development and your personal qualities. To thrive in this role, you will need to demonstrate the following:

Mandatory qualifications and conditions

Due to this role having access to sensitive data, a criminal history check will beundertaken prior to appointment to the role.

Requirements of the role

Proven experience in interpreting and analysing large, complex data sets to prepare spreadsheets, dashboards, reports and presentations in an easy-to-read format. Including a high degree of proficiency in data analysis tools and software is essential, particularly Microsoft Excel.
Demonstrated experience in driving continuous improvement and providing innovative solutions in systems and processes related to reporting.
Highly developed stakeholder engagement and relationship management skills, with the ability to work collaboratively with internal stakeholders across the department and external partners, including Queensland Treasury and the Department of State Development, Infrastructure and Planning.

Some of the additional benefits this opportunity offers include:

Work/life balance, including 'flex time' and flexible work arrangements and a working from home option.
City location close to active and public transport, with just over a 10 minute walk from Central Station.
Work with a supportive team, with a high level of training and development invested in each staff member
Generous leave and superannuation 

For more information on the full list of responsibilities, requirements of the role and how to apply for this exciting opportunity, please read the Role Description and Applicant Guide.

Sara Gaffney can be contacted on 07 •••• •215 should you have any questions regarding this role. 

Applications to remain current for 12 months
